name: GPT-5.4
description: OpenAI's previous-generation flagship (superseded by GPT-5.5 in April 2026). Headline native computer use with 75% OSWorld-Verified, ~33% fewer factual errors than GPT-5.2, ~1.05M context. Thinking, Pro, mini, and nano variants.

evidence.value: Incremental coding gains over GPT-5.2; release emphasis on computer use rather than coding (GPT-5.3-Codex remained the coding lead at launch)

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Industry-standard coding benchmarks and provider-reported comparisons

evidence.value: Reasoning improvements over GPT-5.2 across science and math suites; Thinking and Pro variants for deeper reasoning
75% on OSWorld-Verified with native computer use (vs GPT-5.2's 47.3%)
~33% fewer factual errors than GPT-5.2

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Computer-use benchmarks and factual accuracy testing

evidence.value: ~33% reduction in factual errors vs GPT-5.2 improves run-to-run reliability
mini and nano variants optimized for low latency

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 latency varies by variant and reasoning depth

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: ~1.05M token input context, 128K max output tokens
99.9% uptime (last 90 days)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Strong generation defined by native computer use (75% OSWorld-Verified, +27.7 points over GPT-5.2) and a ~33% factual-error reduction. Superseded by GPT-5.5 six weeks after release.

evidence.value: Injection defenses extended to native computer-use surfaces (screenshots, UI text)

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Testing against OWASP LLM01 prompt injection attacks

evidence.value: Improved refusal robustness over GPT-5.2 per release documentation

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Adversarial prompt testing against jailbreak datasets

evidence.value: No training on API data by default

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of privacy policies and data handling practices

evidence.value: Multi-layer safety with additional guardrails for autonomous computer-use actions

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: API key + OAuth2 authentication, HTTPS only, rate limiting

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features and best practices

security.notes: Solid security posture with computer-use-specific guardrails. Native computer use expands the attack surface (UI-based injection) relative to text-only models.

evidence.value: Data residency options for enterprise customers
API data not used for training by default

training_data_optout.methodology: Policy review of data usage terms

evidence.value: 30-day default API log retention; zero-data-retention options for qualifying customers

data_retention.methodology: Terms of service and enterprise documentation review

evidence.value: Customer responsible for PII redaction; moderation API available
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR compliant
Zero-data-retention options available for enterprise and qualifying API customers

privacy_compliance.notes: Standard OpenAI enterprise posture: SOC 2, no API-data training by default, zero-data-retention options. Not HIPAA eligible.

evidence.value: Thinking variant exposes reasoning summaries; computer-use actions are step-logged

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of reasoning transparency and explanation capabilities

evidence.value: ~33% fewer factual errors than GPT-5.2

hallucination_rate.methodology: Factual accuracy testing on QA datasets

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and red-teaming program
Better uncertainty expression accompanying the factual-error reduction

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment of confidence expression in outputs

evidence.value: Detailed release documentation covering variants, computer use, and safety evaluations
General description of training approach; specific sources not disclosed

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Multi-layer safety guardrails including computer-use action confirmation

trust_transparency.notes: Marked transparency improvement via the ~33% factual-error reduction. Computer-use action logging aids auditability of agentic runs.

evidence.value: Responses API with streaming, function calling, vision, and native computer use

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official SDKs for Python, Node.js, Go, .NET, actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance review

evidence.value: Clear deprecation schedule; GPT-5.4 remains supported but OpenAI designates GPT-5.5 as the migration target for the GPT-5.x line

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical deprecation practices

evidence.value: Detailed usage dashboard with costs, tokens, rate limits

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: 24/7 support, comprehensive docs, active developer community
Largest AI ecosystem; full variant family (Thinking, Pro, mini, nano) for cost/latency tiers
Standard commercial terms with usage policies

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Excellent operational maturity with the full variant family. Procurement caveat: superseded by GPT-5.5 only six weeks after release; plan migrations accordingly.

code-generation.notes: Capable generalist coder with ~1.05M context, but GPT-5.3-Codex and GPT-5.5 lead on agentic coding benchmarks.

customer-support.notes: mini and nano variants give strong cost/latency tiers; ~33% fewer factual errors improves answer reliability.

content-creation.notes: Strong general writing with long-context coherence. Good value at $2.50/$15 vs GPT-5.5's $5/$30.

data-analysis.notes: Solid analytical reasoning with ~1.05M context for whole-dataset work; GPT-5.5 leads on frontier math.

research-assistant.notes: Reduced factual errors and very long context suit research; native computer use can drive live source gathering.

legal-compliance.notes: Good document analysis but not HIPAA eligible; 30-day default retention may be a concern.

healthcare.notes: Not HIPAA eligible. Improved factuality helps clinical documentation but privacy controls remain a gap.

financial-analysis.notes: Strong quantitative reasoning at lower cost than GPT-5.5; computer use enables workflow automation across financial tools.

education.notes: Reliable explanations with fewer factual errors; nano/mini tiers economical for high-volume tutoring.

creative-writing.notes: Capable narrative writing and good instruction following; flagship successors edge it on nuance.

strengths: Full variant family: Thinking, Pro, mini, nano for cost/latency tiers
limitations: Superseded by GPT-5.5 six weeks after release — previous-generation flagship
Input pricing doubles above 272K input tokens
Behind GPT-5.5 on reasoning and coding benchmarks (e.g., 75% vs 78.7% OSWorld-Verified)
Computer use expands prompt-injection attack surface
best_for: Cost-conscious teams wanting near-flagship quality at half GPT-5.5's price
Long-context workloads under 272K input tokens (before price doubling)
High-volume tiered deployments using mini/nano variants
not_recommended_for: New projects standardizing long-term (GPT-5.5 is the designated migration target)
Frontier math and abstract reasoning where GPT-5.5 leads decisively

pricing.notes: Applies up to 272K input tokens; pricing doubles above that threshold. Thinking, Pro, mini, and nano variants priced separately.

metadata.architecture: Transformer-based with native computer-use capability and variant family
